title: 'A more arbitrary and extreme regime': In the West Bank, Israel toughens death penalty law for Palestinians
subhed: A coalition of Israeli and Palestinian non-profit organizations has called for the cancellation of the 'military order' that brought the measure into force, expanding the crimes punishable by death and reducing the means of defense.

title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
French lawmakers back Macron choice to run central bank
Former Arafat adviser faces deportation from France
subhed: Ramy Shaath, a former Palestinian Authority official, is under deportation proceedings due to several radical public positions he has taken since the start of Israel's ground offensive in Gaza, notably through the group he co-founded, Urgence Palestine.

title: Historic buildings in Normandy's Honfleur on verge of collapse
subhed: Along the port city's old harbor, two evacuated buildings threaten to bring down others with them. Traffic is banned, and 19 businesses have shuttered. The lack of maintenance and alterations made by owners of seasonal rentals are being blamed.

title: Robin Campillo, filmmaker: 'I don't see how a blacklist of French cinema could be enforced. Would it even be legal?'
subhed: The director is among the latest figures to sign the open letter criticizing conservative billionaire Vincent Bolloré, now backed by more than 2,000 industry professionals. He emphasizes the importance of unity, one year ahead of the 2027 French presidential election.

title: Jeremy D. Popkin, historian: 'Debates on slavery began decades before the French Revolution'
subhed: The American scholar traces the legal and intellectual history of abolitionism in France, showing how the question of slavery influenced the trajectories of many leading figures of the revolutionary movement.

In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
subhed: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

title: The EU's unflinching collaboration with Libya and Tunisia to contain the flow of migrants
subhed: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (2/4). Giorgia Meloni's government, backed by the European Union, is funding, equipping and training both the Libyan and Tunisian coast guards – even though these forces have committed rights abuses – to intercept people in exile on their way to Europe.

title: Canal+ 'blacklist' threat fuels tensions in the French film industry
subhed: The number of signatories to the anti-Bolloré open letter continues to grow as the CNC, France's national film board, tries to ease tensions and many producers, actors and directors defended Canal+'s work.
